meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Escape Basic App Cycles
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Learn professional project management using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch complex, portfolio-ready software projects today.
content.answer_block: Breaking out of the 'tutorial hell' cycle requires shifting from code-first to architecture-first development. By utilizing structured project management frameworks, you decompose complex features into manageable modules. Using Antigravity for planning and Claude Code for rapid execution allows you to build production-grade applications that demonstrate real engineering maturity.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks churning out 'To-Do' list clones and basic landing pages, yet the jump to a real-world, functional SaaS feels like climbing a mountain in the dark. The frustration is palpable—you have the syntax down, but you lack the framework to transform a nebulous idea into a shippable product. You aren't failing; you are simply lacking a professional blueprint.

Today, we pivot. We are moving beyond simple scripts into the realm of professional engineering. By integrating Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for accelerated implementation, you will bridge the gap between 'student coder' and 'AI Builder'. This is the moment your portfolio stops looking like a collection of exercises and starts looking like a business.

steps.title: Define the Product Scope
steps.description: Stop coding blindly. Define the core 'Value Proposition' of your project to ensure you aren't over-engineering features that don't matter.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lean product requirements document for a [Project Name]. Focus on three core features that provide maximum user value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./requirements.md --ask 'Summarize the MVP scope and suggest one potential technical bottleneck.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Model
steps.description: Structure your database schema before writing a single line of application logic to prevent future refactoring nightmares.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a [Project Name] including user authentication, data tables, and relationships.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate prisma schema based on the provided data model definition.'

steps.title: Establish the Tech Stack
steps.description: Select tools that optimize for speed without sacrificing scalability, ensuring your app can grow as you learn.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Recommend a modern, beginner-friendly tech stack for a SaaS application that minimizes DevOps overhead.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a project scaffold with the recommended stack and configure environment variables.'

steps.title: Implement Modular Logic
steps.description: Break complex features into isolated modules to maintain clean code and simplify debugging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Decompose the user authentication module into discrete, testable functions suitable for a modular architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the authentication service using the modular design pattern defined in the architecture.'

steps.title: Iterative Quality Assurance
steps.description: Use automated testing to ensure that new features don't break your existing codebase as you scale.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the primary business logic functions to ensure edge case coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run tests and fix any failing assertions in the current module.'

pro_tips: Always build the 'Happy Path' first; perfection is the enemy of a working prototype.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it, not after, to keep technical debt at zero.
Limit your MVP to a single, high-impact feature to avoid the 'Feature Creep' death spiral.
Learn to read the error logs before asking the AI; understanding the failure is 50% of the learning.
Commit your code to GitHub every time you finish a discrete task to build a visible, timestamped history of your progress.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is functional, focus on deploying it to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway to get your first real-world user feedback.
content.conversion_block: Don't let another idea die in a local folder. You have the tools, the blueprint, and the expertise to build something that matters. Stop waiting for permission to be a developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
